mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or inflation-adjusted equity valuations do not contract.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals strong economic demand and accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or directionally inverted in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE decline) coupled with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) increases corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 100k, or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate margins stabilize.
failure_condition: INDPRO's predictive power for 60-day SPX returns is invalidated by a Rank IC < 0.05 or negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer demand and wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through increased construction sector activity.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 35% of HOUST growth periods, or the coefficient on HOUST in 20-day return regressions is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals pricing power and demand resilience, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days due to earnings volatility.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to explain SPX returns (R-squared < 0.02) or produces a positive coefficient in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as real rates rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods meeting the trigger conditions, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
